caption: Overview of our proposed framework architecture.
content: Our framework consists of three main modules: (1) a feature extractor based on ResNet-50 that processes input images into 2048-dimensional feature vectors, (2) a transformer-based attention module that captures long-range dependencies between features, and (3) a classification head that maps the attended features to output predictions. The feature extractor first applies convolution layers to extract local features, which are then passed through the attention module. The attention module uses multi-head self-attention with 8 heads to model global relationships. Finally, the classification head uses a two-layer MLP with ReLU activation to produce the final output.
visual_intent: Overview of our proposed framework architecture.
target_diagram_desc0:
The figure should be a clean, schematic architectural diagram illustrating the end-to-end flow of data through the proposed framework. The background is pure white to ensure clarity and focus on the components. All elements are arranged horizontally from left to right in a linear pipeline format, with consistent spacing and alignment to convey a clear progression of processing stages.

At the far left, an input image icon is depicted as a stylized square (100×100 pixels) with a soft gray border and a light pastel blue gradient fill (from #e3f2fd at the top to #bbdefb at the bottom). Inside the square, there is a simplified grayscale illustration of a landscape scene (e.g., mountains, sky, and trees), rendered in a low-resolution, semi-transparent style to suggest visual content without distracting detail. A thin black arrow (width: 1.5pt) extends from the right edge of this image box toward the next component.

The first module, labeled "Feature Extractor (ResNet-50)", is represented as a vertically stacked rectangular block group. This block has a light coral fill color (#ffebee) and a dark gray border (stroke width: 2pt). It consists of three horizontally aligned sub-blocks within it:  
- The top sub-block is labeled "Convolutional Layers" in bold black font (size: 10pt), with a small icon of overlapping circles (symbolizing convolution kernels) inside.  
- The middle sub-block is labeled "Intermediate Blocks" in regular black font (size: 9pt), and is slightly smaller in height than the others.  
- The bottom sub-block is labeled "Global Average Pooling" in bold black font (size: 10pt), with a small downward-pointing triangle icon.  

Each sub-block has a soft drop shadow (opacity: 0.15, blur radius: 4px) to give a subtle sense of depth. The entire feature extractor block outputs a 2048-dimensional feature vector, symbolized by a narrow vertical rectangle (height: 60px, width: 15px) filled with a light lavender color (#f3e5f5), centered below the output side of the ResNet block. Above this vector, the label “2048-d Feature Vector” is written in italicized, dark gray text (size: 9pt).

A thick, solid black arrow (width: 2.5pt) connects the feature vector to the second module. This arrow curves gently upward before entering the next block.

The second module, the "Attention Module", is illustrated as a large horizontal rectangular block with rounded corners (radius: 8px), filled with a light mint green color (#e8f5e8), and outlined with a dark green border (stroke width: 2.5pt). The block contains eight parallel horizontal lines inside, each representing one attention head. Each line is composed of two connected nodes:  
- A circular node on the left, filled with light teal (#b2ffff), labeled “Query/Key/Value”, and  
- A circular node on the right, filled with light yellow (#ffffcc), labeled “Attention Output”.  
These nodes are connected by a bidirectional arrow (length: 40px, thickness: 1.2pt), indicating self-attention computation. Between the nodes, a small diamond-shaped icon represents the attention scoring mechanism. All eight such pairs are evenly spaced vertically within the block and grouped under a central label: “Multi-head Self-Attention (8 heads)” in bold black font (size: 11pt), positioned above the cluster.

An additional annotation near the top of this block states “Softmax + Weighted Sum” in smaller, italicized gray text (size: 8pt), showing the core operation performed across heads.

Another thick black arrow (width: 2.5pt) exits the right side of the attention module, leading into the final component.

The third module, the "Classification Head", is shown as a simple two-layer MLP structure. It is a light orange rectangular block (#fff3e0) with a thin brown border (stroke width: 2pt), containing two nested layers:  
- The first layer is a wide horizontal bar labeled “Hidden Layer (MLP)”, with a ReLU activation icon (a sharp V-shaped curve) placed beside it. This bar is colored in light amber (#ffeb3b) with a darker outline.  
- The second layer, below the first, is labeled “Output Layer” and is a thinner horizontal bar filled with pale red (#ffcdd2).  
Between the two layers, a short dashed arrow indicates forward propagation. A small legend in the bottom-right corner of this block reads: “ReLU → Linear”, using a sans-serif font (size: 8pt).

From the output layer, a final thick arrow (width: 2.5pt) points to the right, terminating in a small circle labeled “Predictions” in black text (size: 10pt), indicating the final classification output.

All arrows throughout the diagram use a consistent design: solid black lines with filled arrowheads (size: 6pt), and no bends except where necessary for layout. Component labels are placed directly above or beside their respective blocks in bold, black, sans-serif font (size: 10–11pt), ensuring readability. The overall layout is balanced and symmetrical, with all modules evenly spaced along a horizontal axis. Margins are generous (at least 20px from edges), and no overlapping occurs between elements. The visual style is minimalistic yet informative, using color coding (coral → mint green → orange) to differentiate stages and maintain cognitive continuity.

The design adheres to NeurIPS 2025’s "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic, with high-value backgrounds and desaturated fills, emphasizing clarity and narrative flow. Rounded containers, orthogonal connectors, and consistent typography reinforce precision and professionalism.
target_diagram_critic_suggestions0:
The diagram description is largely accurate and aligns well with the Methodology Section and Figure Caption. However, there are a few critical issues that need correction to ensure fidelity and clarity:

1. **Feature Vector Representation**: The description states that the 2048-dimensional feature vector is a 'narrow vertical rectangle' centered below the ResNet block. This is inconsistent with typical architectural diagrams where feature vectors are represented as horizontal or inline arrows/rectangles directly connected to the output. Placing it vertically below may mislead readers into thinking it's a separate output stream rather than a direct forward pass.

2. **Attention Module Detailing**: The attention module describes eight parallel lines each with two nodes (Query/Key/Value → Attention Output), but the label 'Multi-head Self-Attention (8 heads)' suggests this should represent 8 attention heads. However, each head typically computes query, key, value, and then attention scores—this is correctly shown—but the depiction of 'Query/Key/Value' in one node is misleading because these are usually three distinct projections. The correct interpretation should show separate Query, Key, and Value components for each head, even if simplified. The current labeling conflates them.

3. **Arrow Flow from Feature Extractor**: The description mentions a thin black arrow extending from the image box to the feature extractor, which is correct. But the subsequent thick arrow from the feature vector to the attention module must originate from the feature extractor block, not from a standalone vector below it. This creates visual confusion about data flow.

4. **Legend Placement**: The description includes a small legend ('ReLU → Linear') inside the classification head. While informative, such internal legends are redundant when labels are already present on layers and can clutter the design. Moreover, it’s not standard practice in NeurIPS-style diagrams to include explicit legends within blocks unless necessary.

5. **Missing Activation Label in MLP**: The ReLU activation icon is mentioned, but no text label for 'ReLU' appears beside it. In clean diagrams, icons should be paired with text labels (e.g., 'ReLU') for accessibility and clarity.

6. **Color Consistency**: The colors used are appropriate per the 'Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels' theme, but consistency in stroke widths across all blocks (especially between 2pt and 2.5pt) should be reviewed. The feature extractor uses 2pt, while attention uses 2.5pt—this slight difference might break visual harmony.

7. **Caption Exclusion**: No caption text ('Figure 1: Overview...') is included in the image, so this rule is satisfied.

Suggested Fixes:
- Move the 2048-d feature vector to be horizontally aligned and directly connected via arrow from the bottom of the ResNet block to the attention module.
- Clarify the attention module by either splitting the 'Query/Key/Value' node into three smaller nodes or using a more abstract representation with clear labels indicating Q, K, V inputs.
- Remove the internal legend ('ReLU → Linear') and instead label the hidden layer explicitly with 'ReLU activation'.
- Add a textual label next to the ReLU icon (e.g., 'ReLU').
- Ensure all arrows are consistently styled (e.g., use 2.5pt thickness throughout for main flows).

These changes will improve technical accuracy and visual clarity without altering the core architecture.

- A circular node on the left, filled with light teal (#b2ffff), labeled “Q”,
- A central circular node, filled with light yellow (#ffffcc), labeled “K/V”,
- A circular node on the right, filled with light lime (#ccffcc), labeled “Attention Output”.

These nodes are connected by bidirectional arrows (length: 40px, thickness: 1.2pt), indicating self-attention computation. - The first layer is a wide horizontal bar labeled “Hidden Layer (MLP)”, colored in light amber (#ffeb3b) with a darker outline. To its right, a sharp V-shaped curve icon (representing ReLU) is placed, with the text label "ReLU" in small black sans-serif font (size: 8pt) next to it.
- The second layer, below the first, is labeled “Output Layer” and is a thinner horizontal bar filled with pale red (#ffcdd2).

Between the two layers, a short dashed arrow indicates forward propagation. The internal legend 'ReLU → Linear' has been removed to avoid redundancy; the activation type is now indicated explicitly.

From the output layer, a final thick arrow (width: 2.5pt) points to the right, terminating in a small circle labeled “Predictions” in black text (size: 10pt), indicating the final classification output.
